planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify the nature and impact of near-perfect feature correlations on dataset structure and model robustness.
key_checks: Assess if correlated features are exact duplicates or derived from common base features
Evaluate dependency concentration profiles to distinguish natural definitions from redundancy
Examine class-conditioned stability of these correlations to detect conditional artifacts
Investigate potential shortcut-like predictive leverage from redundant pairs
success_criteria: Identification of whether strong dependencies arise from feature construction or data duplication
Evidence of how redundancy influences feature selection and model sensitivity
Detection of any weakening or strengthening of correlations across classes
router_constraints: preserve feature pairs with strong correlations
preserve evidence references e13 to e22

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Understand the implications of destination port distribution concentration on attack representation and model generalizability.
key_checks: Verify the zero entropy and dominant ratio metrics for destination port in DDoS class
Check for dataset artifacts versus genuine attack targeting signals in port distribution
Analyze distributional divergence impact on class separability and potential overfitting risks
success_criteria: Clear distinction between artifact-driven and attack-driven distribution concentration
Assessment of how this pattern affects model robustness to unseen port distributions
Identification of representation-sensitive vulnerabilities linked to this feature
router_constraints: preserve feature scope for destination port

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Explore the coexistence of near-constant and highly variable features to characterize dataset heterogeneity and measurement effects.
key_checks: Measure cardinality and distribution shifts for low cardinality bulk and flag features
Evaluate class-conditioned structural variation in features with strong distributional shifts
Investigate if low cardinality features reflect saturation or measurement limits
Assess how models might reconcile or leverage heterogeneous feature behaviors
success_criteria: Evidence clarifying whether low cardinality features obscure meaningful variation
Understanding of how heterogeneous feature types influence model interpretability and stability
Identification of potential feature groups requiring distinct handling strategies
router_constraints: preserve feature scope for low cardinality and variable features
preserve evidence references e8 to e12 and e23 to e33
